today_summary: GCM26 holds $4,354 (DTE 42) with ATM IV at 20.92%, the lowest reading in the 5-session window, as the mid-week flush-and-recover fully digested. PC OI ratio at 0.62 confirms call-side dominance; yesterday's session printed 7,773 call contracts against 3,378 puts (2.3:1) — sustained directional demand above ATM. Max pain at $4,875 sits $521 over spot with 1σ daily move = $57, bracketing today's range to roughly $4,297–$4,411.
five_day_thesis: The 5-day arc is flush, recover, compress: spot cratered from $4,287 (Jun 10) to $4,116 (Jun 12, −4%) as dealer GEX hit −291 on Jun 11 and mechanically amplified every tick lower, then fully reclaimed losses by Jun 16–17. That amplification engine has now almost entirely unwound — aggregate net GEX collapsed from −291 to −7.07, sitting at the threshold of gamma-neutral dealer posture. ATM IV was crushed 2.42pp (23.34% → 20.92%) in the recovery, confirming the dip overpriced fear and rewarding vol sellers. Max pain has drifted down $125 (5000 → 4875) as option writers reprice their neutral zone toward spot, though the $521 residual gap preserves meaningful expiry-pull gravity through the Jun '26 expiry. The $4,000 put wall is the structural constant across all 5 sessions — OI grew from 3,377 to 4,935 contracts (+46%) — marking the institutional floor that held even at the mid-week low. With GEX nearly neutral and IV compressed, the vol regime has shifted from amplifier to stabilizer: expect tighter intraday ranges and drift rather than impulse.
what_changed: Net GEX compressed from −291 (Jun 11) to −7.07 today — dealer posture at gamma-neutral threshold
ATM IV fell 23.34% → 20.92% (−2.42pp) — vol crush confirms dip overpriced tail risk
Max pain drifted $5,000 → $4,875 (−$125) — expiry gravity zone closing toward spot
Spot recovered $238 from the Jun 12 low of $4,116 — flush-and-recover pattern complete
Put wall $4,000 added 46% OI (3,377 → 4,935 contracts) — institutional floor reinforced
GEX flip strike rose 3,310 → 3,485 — dealer support zone migrated $175 higher
prediction: Bias cautiously bullish while spot holds above $4,297 (−1σ daily). Max pain at $4,875 exerts persistent upward pull; the near-term path targets the $4,400 call GEX cluster (call_gex 25.91), then $4,500 as the next OI concentration. Below $4,297 the still-negative GEX regime reactivates as a vol amplifier — avoid longs below that level.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Max pain $521 above spot + 2.3:1 call/put flow. Flip to bearish on sustained break below $4,297.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy consolidation around current spot on any intraday dip into the ATM zone with volume confirmation.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Largest near-ATM call GEX cluster (25.91). Sustained trade above opens path to $4,500 then $4,700. Rejected → fade to $4,330 support.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Below −1σ daily ($57) from entry midpoint. Breaches this and negative GEX re-amplifies downside.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 major call GEX wall — take 60% off. T2 next OI concentration — exit remainder.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: IV 20.92% + GEX nearly neutral → tighter intraday range. Standard size appropriate; 1σ daily = $57.
